evals.prompt: Create a smoke eval for the docs reviewer agent under .agents/docs-reviewer/.evals/ with stable case ids and objective assertions.
evals.expected_output: Creates a compact AgentEvals-style YAML suite beside the target agent with a normalized path and concrete assertions.
expectations: The YAML uses stable case ids and objective assertions

evals.prompt: Make a regression suite for a release-manager agent even though I only asked for an acceptance check.
evals.expected_output: Treats the request as AgentEvals-style coverage and scaffolds a YAML file without needing the user to name AgentEvals explicitly.
expectations: The skill still triggers when the user asks for a smoke test or acceptance check
The scaffold uses one YAML file per eval suite

evals.prompt: Generate a starter eval suite for a QA sweep agent and make the assertions unambiguous enough that a grader can verify them later.
evals.expected_output: Produces a YAML scaffold with concrete case descriptions and assertions that are easy to grade later.
expectations: Assertions are objective rather than vague
The resulting suite is compact and easy to verify
